refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥ 0.97 (97%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as (total scheduled operating time − unplanned downtime) / total scheduled operating time, accounting for all failure modes across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that directly impacts customer value and mission success. Refinement clarifies the measurement methodology, evaluation period, and scope of downtime contributors. High safety criticality requires explicit inclusion of safety-related failures in availability accounting. The metric must be measurable and traceable to subsystem contributions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry logging of operational state transitions (ready, operating, degraded, failed); automated calculation of availability ratio; post-evaluation period audit against customer-defined baseline; root cause analysis of all downtime events exceeding threshold.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall contribute ≤ 0.015 (1.5%) unplanned downtime due to mechanical, electrical, or propulsion failures.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall contribute ≤ 0.010 (1.0%) unplanned downtime due to communication loss, control latency, or operator interface failures.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall contribute ≤ 0.005 (0.5%) unplanned downtime due to safety system faults or false shutdowns.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time availability monitoring and predictive maintenance alerts to prevent cascading failures.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundant subsystem architectures (dual vehicle powertrains, backup teleoperation links, dual safety controllers) with automatic failover to maintain continuous operation.
DP-B: Establish preventive maintenance scheduling and condition-based monitoring with predictive analytics to detect and address degradation before failure.
DP-C: Design modular, replaceable components with rapid swap-out procedures and spare inventory management to minimize mean-time-to-repair (MTTR).
DP-D: Integrate comprehensive telemetry and diagnostics across all subsystems with centralized availability tracking dashboard and automated alerting.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D (Integrated Telemetry & Diagnostics) combined with DP-B (Preventive Maintenance & Condition Monitoring) and DP-C (Modular Design & Rapid Repair). Rationale: DP-D provides the observability and measurement infrastructure required to track and verify the 0.97 availability target. DP-B and DP-C address root causes of downtime (degradation and repair time) without requiring full redundancy (DP-A), which is cost-prohibitive and adds complexity. This combination enables data-driven availability management.

potential_sources: Telemetry system (DP-D) must interface with Vehicle diagnostics (FR-NGHE-004.1 subsystem) and Teleoperation health monitoring (FR-NGHE-004.2 subsystem) without introducing additional failure points.
Preventive maintenance scheduling (DP-B) depends on accurate condition data from telemetry (DP-D); coupling is intentional and manageable.
Modular design (DP-C) must align with telemetry instrumentation points; no conflict if both are designed concurrently.
coupling_check.notes: Selected DPs are loosely coupled at the system level. DP-D is the enabling layer; DP-B and DP-C are complementary strategies. No circular dependencies detected. Decoupling achieved through well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure is reliable and does not itself become a single point of failure. Risk: If telemetry system fails, availability measurement becomes impossible. Mitigation: Redundant telemetry paths and local logging.

Assumption: Predictive maintenance algorithms can accurately forecast failures with sufficient lead time. Risk: False positives (unnecessary maintenance) or false negatives (missed failures). Mitigation: Continuous model refinement and human validation.

Assumption: Spare parts inventory and maintenance personnel are available on-site or nearby. Risk: Supply chain delays or labor unavailability could extend MTTR. Mitigation: Contractual SLAs with maintenance providers.

Assumption: Safety system faults can be distinguished from operational failures. Risk: Safety shutdowns may be counted as downtime, inflating failure metrics. Mitigation: Separate safety-induced downtime from failure-induced downtime in metrics.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-D to explicitly include redundant telemetry paths and local state logging to prevent telemetry system from becoming a critical availability bottleneck. Add DP-E: 'Establish spare parts and maintenance logistics network with guaranteed response time ≤ 4 hours' to support DP-C.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level availability orchestration, telemetry aggregation, diagnostics dashboard, and maintenance scheduling coordination across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Owns the availability KPI measurement, alerting logic, and integration of subsystem health signals.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational state, fault codes, and performance metrics from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Frequency: continuous (≥ 1 Hz). Payload: JSON-encoded vehicle state, sensor readings, error logs.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Transmits critical safety-related availability events and shutdown notifications to Safety Services and Site Manager. Frequency: event-driven. Payload: alert severity, subsystem origin, recommended action.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives maintenance scheduling directives and availability target updates from Site Manager or Maintenance/Quality stakeholder. Frequency: periodic (daily or on-demand). Payload: maintenance windows, target availability thresholds, spare parts status.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for telemetry ingestion and alert distribution. Supports both primary and backup communication paths to ensure availability monitoring is not disrupted.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level owner of availability orchestration, telemetry aggregation, and KPI measurement. Availability is a system-level emergent property that cannot be localized to a single subsystem. High containment: all availability logic, dashboards, and alerting reside here. Low diffusion: minimal leakage to Vehicle or Teleoperation (only sensor/state interfaces).

Vehicle is responsible for its own subsystem availability (FR-NGHE-004.1) but cannot own system-level availability measurement or cross-subsystem diagnostics. Containment limited to vehicle-specific fault detection and local health reporting. High diffusion: availability logic would be scattered across vehicle firmware, teleoperation, and safety systems if assigned here.

Teleoperation is responsible for its own subsystem availability (FR-NGHE-004.2) but is not the appropriate owner of system-level availability orchestration. Containment limited to teleoperation link health and operator interface diagnostics. High diffusion: system-level availability measurement would require Teleoperation to depend on Vehicle and Safety Services data, creating circular dependencies.

Safety Services is responsible for safety-related availability (FR-NGHE-004.3) but not for overall system availability measurement. Containment limited to safety system fault detection and safe shutdown procedures. High diffusion: availability orchestration would violate separation of concerns between safety and operational availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution module owns the system-level availability KPI measurement, telemetry aggregation, diagnostics dashboard, and maintenance scheduling coordination. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules each own their subsystem-level availability contributions (FR-NGHE-004.1, 004.2, 004.3) and report health via Telemetry interface. NGHE Solution consumes these signals and computes the aggregate 0.97 availability metric.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution becomes a single point of failure if telemetry aggregation or availability calculation logic is not redundant. Mitigation: Implement redundant availability monitoring instances with consensus-based KPI calculation.

Risk: Subsystem health reporting (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) may be incomplete or inconsistent, leading to inaccurate availability measurement. Mitigation: Define strict telemetry schema and validation rules; implement health check handshakes.

Risk: Maintenance scheduling logic in NGHE Solution may conflict with safety shutdown procedures in Safety Services. Mitigation: Establish clear priority rules: safety always overrides availability optimization.

Risk: Availability dashboard and alerting may create alert fatigue if not carefully tuned, leading to operator desensitization. Mitigation: Implement intelligent alert filtering and escalation based on severity and impact.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the telemetry schema and validation rules for subsystem health reporting. Establish a formal interface contract between NGHE Solution and each subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) specifying required health signals, update frequency, and fault classification. Consider implementing a separate 'Availability Monitoring' module as a specialized instance within NGHE Solution to isolate KPI calculation from operational tasking.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (integrated telemetry, preventive maintenance, modular design) directly addresses the FR requirement to achieve ≥ 0.97 availability. DP-D provides measurement and observability; DP-B and DP-C address root causes of downtime. No functional gaps identified. Coupling is acceptable and intentional. DPs are implementable within typical heavy equipment constraints.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry aggregation logic (DP-D) is concentrated in NGHE Solution module, creating a single point of failure for availability measurement. Mitigation: Implement redundant telemetry paths and local logging in Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

Preventive maintenance scheduling (DP-B) requires coordination across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Risk of scheduling conflicts or missed maintenance windows. Mitigation: Establish a maintenance task queue with priority arbitration in NGHE Solution.

Modular design and rapid repair (DP-C) depends on spare parts availability and maintenance logistics, which may be external to NGHE Solution module. Risk: IM boundary does not fully control MTTR. Mitigation: Define SLAs with external maintenance providers and include spare parts inventory tracking in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution module appropriately contains system-level availability orchestration, telemetry aggregation, and KPI measurement. Subsystem-level availability contributions (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are owned by respective modules and reported via Telemetry interface. Clear separation of concerns with minimal diffusion. External interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) are well-defined and align with known interface catalog.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Availability KPI is measurable via continuous telemetry logging and post-evaluation analysis. Verification requires: (1) instrumentation of all subsystems to report operational state and fault events, (2) centralized telemetry aggregation in NGHE Solution, (3) automated availability ratio calculation, (4) root cause analysis of downtime events. Feasibility is high; standard practice in industrial equipment monitoring.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-004 is allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level owner. Sub-FRs (004.1, 004.2, 004.3, 004.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively. Allocation is clear and traceable. Each sub-FR has a defined owner and measurable acceptance criterion.

recommended_revisions: Refine the telemetry schema and validation rules to ensure consistent health reporting across subsystems. Define a formal interface contract specifying required signals, update frequency, and fault classification.
Establish explicit SLAs for subsystem availability contributions (FR-NGHE-004.1, 004.2, 004.3) with measurable targets and verification methods.
Define the maintenance scheduling algorithm and priority arbitration rules to prevent conflicts between preventive maintenance and operational tasking.
Implement redundant telemetry paths and local logging to prevent the telemetry system from becoming a single point of failure for availability measurement.
Establish a spare parts inventory management system and maintenance logistics SLAs to support rapid repair (DP-C) and minimize MTTR.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into subsystem-level availability contributions (FR-NGHE-004.1, 004.2, 004.3, 004.4). The refined FR and selected DPs are sound and pass all gates, but the requirement is not atomic and cannot be implemented at this level. Subsystem-level FRs must be further decomposed to identify specific design parameters and implementation modules for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Additionally, the supporting infrastructure (telemetry, maintenance scheduling, spare parts logistics) requires detailed design at the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall contribute ≤ 0.015 (1.5%) unplanned downtime due to mechanical, electrical, or propulsion failures. [Decompose to: powertrain reliability, electrical system redundancy, fault detection and isolation]

FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall contribute ≤ 0.010 (1.0%) unplanned downtime due to communication loss, control latency, or operator interface failures. [Decompose to: communication link redundancy, latency management, operator interface robustness]

FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall contribute ≤ 0.005 (0.5%) unplanned downtime due to safety system faults or false shutdowns. [Decompose to: safety controller redundancy, fault detection, false alarm mitigation]

FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time availability monitoring and predictive maintenance alerts to prevent cascading failures. [Decompose to: telemetry aggregation, diagnostics dashboard, predictive analytics, alerting logic]

assumptions: Telemetry infrastructure is reliable and does not become a single point of failure for availability measurement.
Predictive maintenance algorithms can accurately forecast failures with sufficient lead time (≥ 24 hours).
Spare parts inventory and maintenance personnel are available on-site or nearby with guaranteed response time ≤ 4 hours.
Safety system faults can be distinguished from operational failures in availability metrics.
Customer-defined evaluation period is ≥ 30 days to provide statistically significant availability data.
Subsystem health reporting is consistent and complete across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
Maintenance scheduling does not conflict with operational tasking or safety procedures.
open_risks: Telemetry system failure could prevent availability measurement and mask underlying subsystem failures.
Predictive maintenance false positives could lead to unnecessary downtime and increased maintenance costs.
Supply chain delays or labor unavailability could extend MTTR and violate availability targets.
Safety shutdowns may be incorrectly counted as operational failures, inflating downtime metrics.
Availability measurement methodology may not align with customer expectations or industry standards.
Subsystem-level availability targets (004.1, 004.2, 004.3) may be unachievable with current technology or cost constraints.
evidence_needed: Detailed telemetry schema and validation rules for subsystem health reporting.
Subsystem-level availability targets and verification methods (FR-NGHE-004.1, 004.2, 004.3).
Predictive maintenance algorithm design and validation data (accuracy, lead time, false positive rate).
Spare parts inventory model and maintenance logistics SLAs.
Safety shutdown classification rules and impact on availability metrics.
Customer-defined evaluation period and acceptance criteria for availability measurement.
Benchmark data from comparable heavy equipment systems (industry availability standards).
Cost-benefit analysis of redundancy (DP-A) vs. preventive maintenance (DP-B) and modular design (DP-C).
